message:
Update beads issue tracker with recovery admission control and supervisor improvements

- Add new bead for single-flight recovery admission control, backoff,
  and loop suppression to prevent mailbox supervisor thrashing
- Update existing beads with refined dependency relationships and
  status tracking for the mailbox recovery subsystem

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","title":"Add single-flight recovery admission control, backoff, and loop suppression","description":"Prevent the mailbox supervisor from thrashing on repeated suspect/recovery transitions or launching competing rebuild attempts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:48.348561832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.278041444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:48.348561832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:48.883231856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.276836357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:49.087475052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1324,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should make recovery stable under repeated pressure.

It should specify and eventually implement:
- single-flight recovery ownership
- bounded retry/backoff strategy
- suppression of endless rebuild loops when recovery keeps failing for the same reason
- escalation thresholds when automatic self-healing is no longer making progress

Without this bead, a mailbox can oscillate forever while still technically following the state machine.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","title":"Continuously monitor mailbox health and launch preemptive recovery before user-visible failure","description":"Add proactive runtime drift and suspect-state detection so archive-ahead, empty-DB, deleted-owner, and stale-health conditions trigger safe background recovery before ordinary users notice.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:24.522372846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:25.915895073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:24.522372846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:25.330229300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:24.917266211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:25.115779743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1329,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead makes the durability system proactive instead of merely reactive. It should define periodic health sampling, freshness windows, suspicion thresholds, and when the supervisor is allowed to start a quiet background rebuild before any user-facing command observes a broken mailbox.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","title":"Preserve read-your-writes semantics for accepted writes during degraded recovery","description":"Specify and implement how accepted deferred or archive-first writes remain visible to the originating user, API surface, and later readers while SQLite is degraded or rebuilding, without lying about durability state.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:32.177227214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:34.691451738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:32.177227214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:32.670388900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:32.907011275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:33.155848299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1345,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.12","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead exists for trust. If the system accepts a write during recovery, users should not see that action vanish from subsequent reads just because SQLite is rebuilding. The plan should define pending-write overlays, durability labels, reconciliation rules, and exactly when a surface may say 'accepted but still replaying' versus showing the fully materialized result.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:26:34Z"}]}
+{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.13","title":"Define latency, IO, and cancellation budgets for background or preemptive recovery","description":"Specify how proactive and background rebuild work is rate-limited, deprioritized, paused, or canceled so self-healing does not degrade foreground reads, writes, or operator interactions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:28:25.718379894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:29:35.550949272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.13","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:28:25.718379894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.13","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:28:26.189323554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1347,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.13","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead is about invisible quality. Background recovery should help users, not steal latency from them. The plan should define CPU and IO budgets, pause or cancel rules under foreground pressure, how accepted writes are prioritized relative to rebuild work, and what telemetry proves the system is healing quietly instead of becoming the new source of slowness.","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:29:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","title":"Centralize mailbox health verdict computation in the DB layer","description":"Build one reusable health-verdict engine consumed by startup, doctor, CLI, and server code.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02.856048886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.330737031Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02.856048886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.329521443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1282,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead should combine:
- SQLite integrity facts
- `sqlite_master` / schema presence
- archive inventory
- archive head stamps
- any persisted rebuild metadata

It must distinguish empty-but-valid SQLite from healthy/current SQLite, and separate archive-ahead, suspect, and broken cases cleanly enough to drive policy without string matching.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","title":"Detect split-brain owners, deleted executables, and stale live processes before mutation","description":"Treat active mailbox ownership and deleted-binary drift as correctness signals before recovery starts.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.477766910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.991087687Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.477766910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03.989862621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1283,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Before any mutating recovery path runs, detect:
- whether another Agent Mail process already owns the mailbox
- whether that process is running a deleted executable
- whether the situation requires supervised restart instead of competing recovery

The product should stop treating these cases as weird operator trivia. They are part of the correctness envelope.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","title":"Serve all read surfaces from archive snapshots when live SQLite is suspect","description":"Route read-only surfaces through truthful archive-backed snapshots whenever live SQLite is not trustworthy.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.170007572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.749061321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.170007572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04.747829283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1284,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead is about user invisibility and correctness.

When the mailbox verdict says SQLite is stale, suspect, empty, or broken, read surfaces should keep serving truthful data from archive-backed temp snapshots instead of:
- mutating the live mailbox
- reporting misleading emptiness
- surfacing scary but avoidable failures

Covered surfaces should eventually include CLI, server, web, robot, and TUI reads.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.1","title":"Define ephemeral-root classification and default-mailbox safety policy","description":"Specify which roots and execution contexts count as ephemeral and what the safe default behavior is for each.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08.254177117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08.627903437Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08.254177117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1288,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead must explicitly classify high-risk contexts such as:
- `tmp`
- `var/tmp`
- `dev/shm`
- explicit test harnesses
- repro dirs
- synthetic agent session paths

It should define default behavior, override behavior, and expected diagnostics for each class.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.2","title":"Auto-reroute tmp/dev/shm/test runs to isolated storage roots","description":"Implement the safe default by deriving isolated storage roots automatically for ephemeral runs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08.814925836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:09.430942297Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:08.814925836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:09.429607986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1289,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"The derived isolated storage path should be:
- automatic where feasible
- predictable enough to debug later
- strong enough to eliminate accidental writes into the default operator mailbox for covered entrypoints
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.3","title":"Require explicit unsafe opt-in before ephemeral runs touch the shared mailbox","description":"Add a deliberate override path for exceptional cases where ephemeral runs must touch the shared mailbox.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:09.623210825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:10.240688514Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:09.623210825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:10.239432170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1290,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"The safe default must remain unchanged unless someone explicitly opts into the risk.

This bead should make unsafe opt-in:
- deliberate
- clearly signaled in diagnostics/logs
- easy to audit after the fact
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:10Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","title":"Propagate storage-root isolation through wrappers, scripts, installers, and E2E harnesses","description":"Apply the same isolation policy to operational entrypoints that would otherwise bypass the safe default.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:10.442202828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:07.084998900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:10.442202828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:11.068809824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:11.319384378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:07.083783873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1291,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"This bead exists so the policy does not stop at core code.

Apply the same isolation behavior to wrappers, helper scripts, installers, test harnesses, and convenience entrypoints. There should be no obvious side door that still contaminates the default mailbox accidentally.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:10Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.5","title":"Verify ephemeral workloads cannot contaminate the operator mailbox archive","description":"Build the proof that safe-default isolation prevents transient workloads from writing into the operator mailbox.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:11.521395633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:07.274733634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:11.521395633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:12.154785619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.5","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:24:07.273626660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1292,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verification should cover both:
- safe-default behavior
- explicit unsafe-override behavior

The goal is to catch future regressions automatically instead of rediscovering contamination operationally.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","title":"Durability program: capture forensic incident bundles before repair/reconstruct","description":"Capture structured forensic bundles before any repair, reconstruct, or automatic recovery mutates mailbox state.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02.276122582Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","db","doctor","forensics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1280,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Intent

Preserve enough evidence to explain why a mailbox entered recovery before any quarantine, repair, reconstruct, or candidate promotion step mutates the database family.

The system should not force operators to choose between restoring service and preserving diagnosable evidence.

## Bundle Doctrine

A forensic bundle is a first-class product artifact. It should capture:
- the live DB family and sidecars
- archive drift facts
- lock/process state
- environment/config summary
- candidate validation outputs

## Why This Matters

The forensic bundle is the bridge between invisible self-healing and later root-cause analysis. Without it, every incident turns back into guesswork.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-9jl","title":"CLI: share verify/decrypt parity (signature + age)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share verify` and `share decrypt` with legacy semantics.

## Scope
- `share verify`:
  - Validate SRI hashes and optional Ed25519 signature.
  - `--public-key` uses provided base64 key; else use manifest signature.
- `share decrypt`:
  - `--output` defaults to filename sans `.age` (or `_decrypted`).
  - `--identity` (age key file) is mutually exclusive with `--passphrase`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and decrypt flag validation.
- Integration tests decrypting a bundle produced by share export.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store verification results under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_verify/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share verify/decrypt match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. Signature validation and age decrypt succeed on valid inputs and fail loudly on invalid.
3.
